Module 4 – Derived Photometric Parameters

euclid_pipelines.euclid_module4(profiles, object_id, outPath, z=None)[source]

Euclid Pipeline 4. Measure the resolved photometry on the surface brightness profiles of different bands.

Result file: EUC_SEGMID_{object_id}_photometry.csv

TODO:

  • Add SB limit like when reaching 1 sigma error.

Parameters:

  • profiles (Table) – Table with the profiles of the source in different bands

  • object_id (int) – Euclid’s object ID of the source

  • outPath (str) – Path to save the photometry

Returns:

done – Success status of the pipeline

Return type:

bool

Scientific description

Module 4 converts the surface brightness profiles from M3 into a set of physically meaningful integrated and structural parameters for each photometric band. The methodology follows the S⁴G (Spitzer Survey of Stellar Structure in Galaxies; :cite:t:`Seth10`; :cite:t:`Munoz-Mateos13`) and CS⁴G (:cite:t:`Sanchez-Alarcon25`) pipelines.

Asymptotic magnitude

Total magnitudes are derived from the curve of growth (CoG), i.e. the cumulative flux as a function of aperture radius. Because the CoG does not always reach a true plateau (faint flux continues to accumulate), the asymptotic magnitude is obtained by fitting a straight line to the final, settling portion of the CoG and extrapolating to its y-intercept (:cite:t:`Watkins22`).

Structural radii

The following radii are measured for each band:

Parameter

Definition

\(R_\mathrm{eff}\) (reff)

Half-light (effective) radius enclosing 50 % of the asymptotic flux.

\(R_{25.5}\), \(R_{26.5}\), \(R_{28}\) (r25p5, r26p5, r28)

Isophotal radii at surface brightness levels of 25.5, 26.5, and 28 mag arcsec⁻²; also measured with inclination correction, redshift dimming, and both corrections combined.

\(R_\mathrm{petro}\) (rpetro)

Petrosian radius.

At each isophotal radius the covariant PA and \(\varepsilon\) are also recorded.

Surface brightness at the effective radius

The mean surface brightness within \(R_\mathrm{eff}\) is:

\[\langle \mu \rangle_\mathrm{eff} = m_\mathrm{tot} + 2.5\log_{10}(2\pi R_\mathrm{eff}^2) \,,\]

where \(m_\mathrm{tot}\) is the asymptotic magnitude and all quantities are in consistent units.

Concentration indices

Two non-parametric morphology proxies are measured:

  • \(C_{82} = 5\log_{10}(R_{80}/R_{20})\) — ratio of the radii enclosing 80 % and 20 % of the total flux.

  • \(C_{31} = R_{75}/R_{25}\) — ratio of the radii enclosing 75 % and 25 %.

Inclination and redshift corrections

Inclination-corrected radii are derived using the outer axis ratio \((b/a) = 1 - \varepsilon_\mathrm{out}\), where \(\varepsilon_\mathrm{out}\) is the median ellipticity at radii beyond \(R_{25.5}\). The inclination correction applied to surface brightness is:

\[\Delta \mu_\mathrm{incl} = -2.5\log_{10}(1 - \varepsilon_\mathrm{out}) \,.\]

A redshift dimming correction \(\Delta \mu_\mathrm{dim}\) is also applied when the source redshift \(z\) is known. Corrected isophotal radii are provided for three cases: inclination only, dimming only, and both.

Output file

File

Description

EUC_SEGMID_<id>_photometry.csv

One-row CSV with object_id and, for each band <b>: mag_<b>, reff_<b>, sb_eff_<b>, c82_<b>, c31_<b>, rpetro_<b>, r25p5_<b>, r26p5_<b>, r28_<b> (plus pa and eps at each isophotal level and their corrected variants), and axisRatio_<b>.
